_comment: Table-mode golden for enriching a SCHEMA-ONLY replica of the public bigquery-public-data.crypto_bitcoin dataset (OKF's cross-table sample, okf/samples/crypto_bitcoin). The source is hundreds of GB and ships rich column descriptions, so instead of copying it we build a schema-only, descriptions-stripped replica in your project with eval/tools/make_crypto_bitcoin_replica.py (so enrichment must come from the grounding corpus, not baked-in schema descriptions). Out-of-the-box flow: build the replica, run the agent in table mode against it grounded by eval/corpora/crypto_bitcoin/, then score with `python -m eval --output-dir <out> --golden goldens/table_crypto_bitcoin.json`. Scoring matches each table by NAME, so the project prefix in your catalog path need not match. The golden_facts below are stated in the grounding corpus (bitcoin-etl schema + GCP 'Bitcoin in BigQuery' docs). Exercises cross-table foreign-key relationships.
_dataset_doc:
Public SOURCE dataset. Too large to copy and its schema ships descriptions; build a schema-only, description-stripped replica in your project with `python eval/tools/make_crypto_bitcoin_replica.py --project

--dataset crypto_bitcoin`, then enrich that replica.
grounding: eval/corpora/crypto_bitcoin (4 local markdown docs: dataset overview, blocks, transactions/UTXO, inputs/outputs) — pass as --folders to the agent.

run._doc:
Runnable CASE for `python -m eval --run --project

`: build the schema-only replica (make_crypto_bitcoin_replica.py), then enrich it in table mode grounded by the local corpus. --folders is relative to agents/enrichment.

golden_facts: holds one row per block in the Bitcoin blockchain, loaded by the open-source bitcoin-etl pipeline
hash is the block's unique identifier and is referenced by transactions.block_hash
number is the block height -- the block's sequential position in the chain (genesis block is 0), referenced by transactions.block_number
merkle_root is the root of the Merkle tree whose leaves are the block's transaction hashes
transaction_count is the number of transactions included in the block
partitioned by timestamp_month (the month of the block creation timestamp)
holds one row per Bitcoin transaction; each transaction belongs to exactly one block
block_hash and block_number reference the containing block (blocks.hash / blocks.number)
follows the UTXO model: nested inputs spend the outputs of prior transactions and nested outputs are newly created spendable outputs
an input's spent_transaction_hash and spent_output_index reference a prior transaction's output
fee equals input_value minus output_value
is_coinbase marks the coinbase transaction, the first transaction in a block that creates new coins (the block reward)
partitioned by block_timestamp_month
a denormalized, one-row-per-input representation of the repeated transactions.inputs[] records
spent_transaction_hash and spent_output_index reference the previous transaction's output being consumed (the UTXO link)
joins to transactions on transaction_hash and to blocks on block_hash / block_number
carries the owning addresses and the value of the spent output
a denormalized, one-row-per-output representation of the repeated transactions.outputs[] records
an output is identified by (transaction_hash, index) and is later referenced by a spending input's (spent_transaction_hash, spent_output_index)
carries the recipient addresses and the value of the output
joins to transactions on transaction_hash and to blocks on block_hash / block_number
